channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Wisconsin reporter gives insight on country’s most expensive judicial race
item.description: More than $90 million has poured into a Wisconsin Supreme Court race that will decide the court’s ideological majority.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Judge says he will order government to preserve Signal messages about Houthi military strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday said he will order the Trump administration to preserve records of a text message chat in which senior national security officials discussed sensitive details of plans for a U.S. military strike against Yemen’s Houthis. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US partners with Colombia to take on immigration using biometric technologies
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem met with Colombia foreign minister Thursday, when the two signed an agreement to deploy biometric capabilities to take on migration and crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Saving Billions
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Foragers want a seat at the rulemaking table as DNR mulls mushroom, berry bag limits
item.description: The DNR is considering bag limits on how much food people can take from state-managed lands. That gallon limit is ringing alarm bells for foraging groups like the Minnesota Mycological Society.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: US economic growth to slow in the next 30 years, fueled by debt and declining birth rates, CBO says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Weak population gains and increased government spending will result in slower overall economic growth over the next 30 years, the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department eyes combining ATF and DEA as part of broad restructuring
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Justice Department leadership is proposing combining the two agencies responsible for enforcing drug and gun laws as part of a dramatic restructuring of the department, according to a memo reviewed by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: 6 Dems vote with House GOP to reverse Biden-era climate rules
item.description: Six House Democrats broke from their party to vote for a pair of energy bills bucking progressive Biden-era policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arizona Senate leader calls for federal meeting on DEI ‘window dressing’ at state colleges
item.description: Arizona state Senate President Warren Petersen, R-Gilbert, is writing Thursday to Education Secretary Linda McMahon, asking for a meeting on ending a DEI crisis in Arizona schools.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shares of GM and other automakers slump as they face higher costs from Trump tariffs
item.description: Automaker stocks slumped following President Donald Trump’s announcement that he will place 25% tariffs on auto imports.
item.dc:creator: Damian J. Troise, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US cities located in states won by Trump would be most hurt by Canadian tariffs, an analysis finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. cities most vulnerable to a trade war with Canada turn out to largely be in the states that helped return Donald Trump to the White House — a sign of the possible political risk he's taking with his tariff plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration revokes state and local health funding
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services is canceling tens of billions of dollars in federal grants that state and local health departments were using to track infectious diseases, health disparities, vaccinations, mental health services, and other health issues.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court won't lift order that barred Trump administration from deportations under wartime law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court refused Wednesday to lift an order barring the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law. A split three-judge panel of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Signal attack plan messages: What we do (and don't) know
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The revelation that President Donald Trump's most senior national security officials posted the specifics of a military attack to a chat group that included a journalist hours before the attack took place in Yemen has raised many questions.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order from U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts to slash the federal bureaucracy. A three-judge panel on the U.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration backtracks on some ID requirements after backlash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration is partially backtracking on a plan that would require all new and existing beneficiaries to travel to a Social Security field office to verify their identity.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NPR and PBS heads answer questions on Capitol Hill regarding allegations of bias
item.description: The CEOs of the largest U.S. public broadcasting networks appeared before a House subcommittee chaired by Republican Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman and David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: In tight budget year, Minnesota program to help homeless seniors faces stiff competition
item.description: Catholic Charities Twin Cities is seeking state funding to help keep a program going to address the growing issue of homelessness among those age 60 and older.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Republican lawmakers seek to put PBS and NPR in the hot seat
item.description: A House subcommittee led by Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene and named after Elon Musk’s government-efficiency team has set its sights on the public broadcasters.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: NYC Council to vote on new rules for scaffolding, sidewalk sheds
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council is set to take up a series of bills aimed at cracking down on scaffolding and sidewalk sheds.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Intelligence leaders: We didn’t share classified information in Signal chat group
item.description: Some of the nation’s top intelligence officials will appear before Congress in a pair of hearings this week. Two were participants in a widely criticized war plans group chat on Signal.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: NPR, PBS defend their 'essential' need during heated DOGE hearing
item.description: At stake were potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump orders declassification of FBI’s Russia investigation
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed a memorandum directing the declassification of FBI files related to the investigation into his 2016 campaign's potential ties to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Why Israel's having some of its biggest protests since the war in Gaza began
item.description: They're demanding a deal between Israel and Hamas to release all the remaining hostages, and also demonstrating against government attempts to weaken the judiciary.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf and Daniel Estrin, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Sanders at North Las Vegas rally says GOP, Dems out of touch with working families
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Vermont Independent Sen. Bernie Sanders and Democratic Rep. New York Congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez held a rally Thursday in North Las Vegas, calling for a new way to defeat President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News
